prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG 1920s cosmic-horror atlas map, first batch pass, landscape orientation
Primary request: Call of Cthulhu / 1920s Lovecraftian inspired world map, dual-continent terrain type.
Scene/backdrop: overhead birds-eye aged parchment atlas view with two fog-shrouded landmasses separated by uneven seas and narrow straits.
Subject: two asymmetric continents divided by a diagonal S-shaped and C-bending cold ocean corridor, not a simple vertical split. Include island-studded straits, inland lakes, misty gulfs, rocky capes, broken shores, marshy river deltas, jagged peninsulas, Arkham-like university towns, Miskatonic-style river delta settlements, old colonial ports, lighthouse coasts, foggy hills, ancient standing stones, forbidden cult sites, graveyards, forests, wetlands, tundra, mountains, and bleak coastal regions.
Style/medium: 1920s New England town map expanded to continental scale, Lovecraftian horror, aged parchment with hand annotations but no readable text, occult cartography, highly detailed illustrated RPG sourcebook map.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape map, both continents fully visible, diagonal/S/C-shaped sea separation, generous dark ocean border, top-down map clarity.
Lighting/mood: creeping cosmic dread, Jazz Age antiquarian mystery, fog, impossible shadows, ancient gods slumbering beneath earth and sea, sanity-eroding atmosphere.
Color palette: aged sepia parchment, cold grey-blue ocean, faded ink, sickly green occult accents, fog grey, dark pine forests, autumn browns.
Materials/textures: brittle paper, ink wash, etched coastlines, faint coffee stains, marginal occult sigils without readable lettering.
Constraints: no text labels, no letters, no readable words, no legend, no compass text, no watermark, no UI, no characters or portraits. Avoid mirrored symmetry and avoid a clean vertical division.
